title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: My mom (who's a doctor) started watching this show but had to stop because it was too similar to her normal work day and she wanted to relax
author: @not-so-good-at-arting

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: Using med students is a super clever narrative device. It lets them explain things to us without sounding like they’re talking about stuff they should know about.
author: @shadowhunter483

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: Neurologist here. BEGGING you to stop using the term "MINI STROKE ". People think you mean they had a stroke that was small. It means they had NO STROKE and it's a diagnosis of exclusion. They are usually MIGRAINES.
author: @SB-pl8fc

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: As a medical social worker, I can’t tell you how wonderful it is to finally see an ER show that doesn’t erase our existence. The portrayal of a social worker that everyone relies on is very accurate.
author: @Chiricahua-11

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: 8:32 blood glucose would be a quick, low cost, and reasonable test. A lot of people don't even know they're diabetic until they have a low sugar faint.
author: @Binturong3850

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: It seems like the only inaccuracies are there for the sake of preserving the time frame for the episodes, which imo is as good as a medical drama can get.
author: @ethapher

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: I’m an ER nurse. It’s cool that Dr. Mike reviewed this, but I don’t think I can watch this show. When I’m off the clock, I need to escape from the hospital, not relive my shifts. However, I think this show could be really helpful for the general public; people have no clue what we go through
author: @hannahtimmons2370

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: You know how in Grey's anatomy everything seems lowkey simple and easy which makes you want to become a doctor? The Pitt feels and looks so real. It shows the difficulty of the ER/Medical Field and you really get the idea of the harships a doctor face in the ER.
author: @crskvl

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body:
The only flaw is EVERYONE wears a name tag saying who is a med student 1,2,or 3rd yr.
Interns, Residents, Attending
author: @msmithrandir561

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: The classic, “I expect you do be better but I am not willing to give you more money, more staff, more resources, or more authority to make improvements. Clearly, I expect you to perform a miracle since there is little you can do to enact meaningful change.”
author: @nathans5773

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: Two things that struck me, they WALK from case to case rarely run. Also, they way they subconsciously pick up gloves, no dramatic glove application
author: @dpiland2

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: I really appreciate your comment about asking about student doctors WITHOUT them present. I remember going to the psychiatrist once as a sophomore in high school, absolutely mortified that I was there, and the doctor brought in a student with them and didn’t even really give me the option to say I wasn’t comfortable. Now as an adult with a better understanding of our medical system, I don’t think I would mind, but as a scared, embarrassed kid it was so uncomfortable.
author: @samoyed7895

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: It's really the small things that make this show amazing, like the fact there is no backround music just hits that realism so much more.
author: @ItzBrittKneeBish

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: It's been a very long time since I've seen an American tv show where all the actors look like normal people, how nice.
author: @littlehaven7275

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: 10:40 is such a good line. I've been given patient surveys before and I'm like "i don't tend to discuss hospital recommendations with my friends..."
author: @strawberrypink.

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body:
I love Dr. King so much. As someone with autism who works as a nurse, I see a lot of myself in her. She’s very socially awkward, yet has a lot of compassion. She gets excited over things some people wouldn’t. She also has small, positive ways of grounding herself while she works when she gets overstimulated or stressed out. It really made me feel seen, as cheesy as it sounds

Something else I love about this show is how supportive the staff is of their nurses. They treat Dana—the charge nurse—with a bunch of respect, and even go into nursing shortages and abuse in the show as well. It’s good to see a medical drama that takes a more realistic stance instead of one that focuses on “which doctor is sleeping with who”
author: @amelonnamedkate1400

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: There’s no way I would’ve understood all these intricacies if I watched it by myself. I demand the show with Dr. Mike’s commentary for all episodes!
author: @adityadn95

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: You can tell how realistic it is cuz Dr. Mike was getting anxious throughout the show. The creators of the show really make you feel like you are there in the E.R. saving lives. Great show. Great video.
author: @smollZzzz

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: 3:15 When I was young my doctor was the head of Pediatric GI at my major hospital. My room would always be 10 interns deep. I never remember him asking before. 😅
author: @igraeb

title: The Most Accurate Medical Drama Of All Time? | The Pitt
body: Okay - you've just sold me on this show. I was a nurse for 15 years, primarily operating theatres, and I'm a sucker for a good medical drama.
author: @philiponeill6903

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: Dr Mike, thank you for the shout out to the set design! I am friends with the PD for this show and she worked tirelessly to make sure that everything looked great. Production roles throughout the entertainment industry don't often get a lot of love, so when the work is noticed, its always appreciated <3
author: @nylonbandit

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: Fun Fact: one of the nurses is an actual ER Nurse. He used to be an actor then focused on his band. when is band broke up he became an actual ER Nurse, and was that for about 8 years
author: @greatleapforwards

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: I jumped with joy at the mention of Hoʻoponopono. When my therapist introduced this concept, it really helped me mourn.
author: @Thelastswablu

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: I think the part that makes it so realistic is the lack of the dramatic music. The scenes are so quiet. It’s just the silence and the sounds of emotions and other patients. That’s so RAW. This episode made me absolutely bawl my eyes out.
author: @LittleFishy219

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: 1:38 "I love you. Thank you. I forgive you. Please forgive me" That's beautiful. I'd never heard about Ho'oponopono (it's a "a traditional Hawaiian practice of reconciliation and forgiveness"), but it really seems like a good way to avoid regret (since it covers pretty much everything). Wikipedia says it is "based on the idea of 100% responsibility, taking responsibility for everyone's actions, not only for one's own. If one would take complete responsibility for one's life, then everything one sees, hears, tastes, touches, or in any way experiences would be one's responsibility because it is in one's life. The problem would not be with one's external reality, it would be with oneself".
author: @pedrostormrage

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: As a physiotherapist, one big mistake I see a lot of Doctors make is completely ignoring the mechanism of injury.  That is one of the most important factors in diagnosing orthopedic injuries.
author: @damonx6109

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: OH BUDDY as a patient who has had a door accidentally opened during a pelvic exam, I SO appreciate you mentioning this. Every caregiver needs to understand there needs to be another layer, or just have the table face away from the door for that bit. And now, as a patient, I am not afraid to say hey, can we do this with a different angle or can someone stand by that door?
author: @jessicaclary298

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: I love that they showed him listening for a heartbeat for a minute instead of just barely listening like most shows do
author: @alaj3395

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body:
I like how they’ve shown the family with the elderly man
It shows how hard it can be to let someone go but it never treats them like the bad guy for desperately wanting to keep him alive at first
author: @kyleglendinning9414

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: My dad passed at 91 a month and eight days ago. The moment the daughter said ‘I thought we would have more time’ broke me (I’m 30). I hadn’t been able to cry until now.
author: @WillowDrown

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: I love how this show proves that you can make a realistoc grounded show that is interesting without having to make stuff up or inject artificial drama
author: @dadmitri4259

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: The "Average person eats 3 spiders a year" factoid is actually a statistical error. The average person eats 0 spiders per year. Spiders Georg, who lives in a cave and eats over 10,000 each day is an outlier and should not have been counted.
author: @EurydiceAllAlong

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: I really love how this show has all these "little" cases that show everyday moments in the ER. I'm a trans guy so I was both touched by the quick and no-fuss way they changed the patient's gender marker and name on her file (and how they called it a "misgendering error", making it clear that it was considered an error of the system and not somehow the patient being duplicitous because she can't get her license / insurance changed to her actual name). Same with the dad being truly caring and supportive of his daughter and her medical issue. I was terrified as a kid / teen when I hadn't had a period at 14 and worried something was wrong. Then turnabout happened and I had incredibly painful periods twice a month for a week each and I lost so much blood I became anemic and was almost emergency dumped into a hospital to get transfusions. Two issues I dealt with in my life just happen in this show to "random" patients and they are treated the same way that any of the other patients are. It's refreshing to not see your issue be treated as a novelty but rather something that can be addressed and fixed without too much fuss :)
author: @MeonLights

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: The numbing medication for the patient with the bug in the ear was to kill the bug. It was wiggling earlier. Another doc I follow who specializes in the ER says they do that when the bug is alive to kill it to prevent further damage to the patient.
author: @Heroesfullyalive

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: I'm studying to get my RN license and struggle with imposter syndrome, thinking I won't retain enough information to be knowledgeable on handling moment to moment patient care. This episode gave me my first confidence boost I've had in months, when Santos ordered the BiPAP for the patient with pnuemothorax I was screaming at my screen "Don't do it, pressurized air can give him tension pneumothorax!". When I turned out to be right I finally felt like maybe I've retained more than I give myself credit for.
author: @FearfulSludgeBoy

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: As a trans woman this was easily the most true-to-life representation I've seen on a medical show. Tasha's transness only comes up because of the misgendering error, which is awkward but obviously something she's used to. My only quibble is that Javadi should have gotten the patient's permission before updating her chart - some trans people may have concerns about privacy and possible discrimination, and sometimes discrepancies between legal and preferred name can mess with your insurance paperwork.
author: @heatherc.7706

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: It would be great if Dr Mike got Noah Wyle on his podcast to talk about the problems medical professionals face, and their portrayal in the show.
author: @Viktor-x1w7w

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: The lack of unnecessary drama really makes it feel real. Those scenes in this episode and the last with the old father going through his last moments were very heart wrenching, because in those moments that's how it is, usually. There's no grandiose music playing, there's no last hurrah, it's just you with a million things you still have to say, but no longer can't, and the deafening silence.
author: @rmg480

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: @1:06, I'm not religious, none of my family is particularly religious.. but having the chaplain come to my father's bedside when he passed was very comforting after an incredibly traumatic experience (lung cancer; non-small cell adenocarcinoma, and folks, don't smoke, wear PPE around hazardous materials, don't screw with asbestos.. it could have been any of those in his 73 years prior to diagnosis, and I would not wish what he went through and I sat with him through on anyone, it has forever changed me as a person).
author: @aaronbuildsa

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: As a RRT, when she ordered the bipap, I shouted “NOOO!” I’m glad they demonstrated what happens with this bad idea.
author: @neox79

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: this is the gold standard of reaction content. Go through a portion of a scene, then explain the what why when and how of everything with it. Gold. Standard.
author: @A_K_James

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: The actress playing Mr Spencers daughter gives such a raw emotionally draining performance saying goodbye to her father. I dont cry but I did after her performance. The loss and regret and finality is so deeply emoted by her. Letting go is so hard. Great performance and writing.
author: @badkneesone

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: The grieving ritual is how I'm talking to my 76 year old mother now. She has Parkinson's with dementia and becomes very combative when we talk, due to a lot of trauma we've both endured. I start our conversations now with: Hey Mom, I love you. I'm so sorry for... Then I go on to apologize first and it opens the doors to anything she wants to say. It's produced a lot of tears but it's also mending some fences. Don't wait until you can't talk to them anymore. Use this technique to grieve while you're still alive, and it works wonders. <3
author: @Ritapita73

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: Do we enjoy watching this show with you because it’s fun? Yes. Do we mainly enjoy watching it because you finally get to be happy about a medically accurate show? Yes! 😂😂😂
author: @MollyKillers

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body:
This past winter, my papa was placed on comfort care. He was 89 and had a second hip fracture and declined steadily after the surgery. We did not place him on ventilation and instead gave him morphine and oxygen. He was then transferred back to his nursing home to be closer to his wife. He passed the next morning. I’m so glad we decided to make him comfortable instead of intubate. Thank you for sharing gently and honestly what it is like to have an elderly person pass at the hospital.

I am forever grateful to his team of nurses and doctors for being there when we couldn’t.
author: @Clementinedad

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body:
please keep going this show is a rollercoaster all the way through.

btw as an autistic person, I just love how they portrayed Mel. they don't explicitly say she has ASD, but they do mention that her sister does, and she exhibits many relatable behaviors herself.
author: @kay4640

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Ep. 4
body: I’m a nanny the hair thing happens more often than you think. Babies pull on hair all the time and it gets tangled in places you don’t even notice. This is even something that happens with boys around there private areas. I always make sure there’s no hair in diapers when I change them
author: @shanoriya

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: My favorite thing about this show is that there's no music. No soundtrack to build tension or suspense. The moments are just happening, and we need to sit with them.
author: @doctor-jw

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: I really liked how they depicted the doctors in this. They often disagree and clash but then move on and continue to work together because they're grown adults with a job to do.
author: @SacredDaturaa

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: My wife and I loved to watch E.R. We named our son Noah, after Noah Wylie. Now its 22 years later and so much has happened since then, including divorce. But we've remained good friends. Sadly, she passed away three days ago from brain cancer. I will never be the same. I discovered this new show and I wish so much that I could watch it with her.
author: @coreyrueckheim3881

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: Physician suicide is real. The fact that you see two scenes, with two different doctors, standing at the edge of a building should be very very very unsettling for everyone. It's common for "frontline" healthcare workers to joke about this kind of thing but imagine any other profession joking about suicide like this. They nailed it with "pay a living wage and nurses will line up". CEO's and their friends are stealing lives from the people who actually make a difference by raking in millions while paying pennies.
author: @msheehandub

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: This is the most accurate depiction of emergency medicine I have ever seen. It’s incredibly accurate.  Even the medical terminology and dosing is accurate.
author: @dwkwb8

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: I'm most excited that this seems like it will be a grounded, more real time look at a night in a busy ER. Less interpersonal character drama and more about the stress of working an insane shift in an ER like this.
author: @Freeden

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: I appreciate that this show not only talks about Covid but shows the PTSD as a result of it. Funny how very few shows, if any, even mention a pandemic that slew millions and keeps disabling ppl even today.
author: @Gleem1313

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: you set the tone carter , i really wished this was a sequel to ER in carters new hospital but im just happy to see noah in med drama again
author: @zuhairkhan5954

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: After working in hospitals for 30 plus years in Administration, I can tell you that a lot of this is very true and ER's are always crazy no matter where the hospital is located. I've witnessed residents cry in hallways during covid, I've seen things in hallways that most ppl never see, even some ghosts on camera's and staff getting attacked by fellow employees (I worked in Security Administration, Nursing Admin and Psych Admin). Hospitals can be a wonderful place to work or your worst nightmare. I'll tell you this, everyone who works in a hospital in any capacity deserves respect.
author: @artbytoniZip

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: The real time nature of this is an exciting aspect. And with no commercials interruptions (something 24 couldn’t avoid) it can actually be 15 real hours. Love the concept.
author: @tgs1766

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: Just watched the finale. What a masterpiece. This show truly captures what being in medicine is like; the ups, the downs, the chaos, the calm, and the humor! I’m also so happy with how the writers spent so much time and effort portraying the vital importance of the nurses, social workers, and technicians. Can’t wait for season 2!
author: @panfrick

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: I swear they snuck cameras into a hospital and got real patients and real family members of dying people cuz the acting is insane. It's like being in a real hospital and having to break the news that a family member died. The family of the overdosed teen and the family of the little girl that drowned really broke me
author: @erikgonzales3178

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: Dr. Carter never won an Emmy - that’s just wrong. His whole arc on ER was soooo good. One of the standout characters that made ER a such a phenomenal show. I wonder if I will be able to switch ER and Carter off in my mind watching this?
author: @Rheinmeister09

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: I showed my mom this Show, and she is in LOVE, shes a nurse who worked years in the ER , and was there a med Student too, and she said that this show is so accurate that it makes her and the medical team feel seen
author: @Handoftheworld

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: One of the most realistic medical series I have ever seen. The situations are new and relatable. I turn off my phone and I hinge on every word when this show comes on. Noah Wiley is a natural in this role. My name’s not Siskel or Ebert, but I definitely give The Pitt two thumbs up.
author: @TheDspain

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body:
Did anyone else for a HOT SECOND think that was Pablo Schreiber?😅

Jokes aside, I love seeing Noah Wyle, this guy is an underrated actor that should be in more stuff. I remember him back during the Falling Sky’s days if anyone wanted a throwback.

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: Finally! The first and only medical show to not only be realistic but also for the first time ever to write nurse characters who are Filipino!
author: @DocVancouver

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: Incredible show, from someone who got shot in the stomach and WAS TAKEN TO THE ER TO HAVE MY LIFE SAVED. Hats off to all the healthcare professionals
author: @kportillo2002

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: I love that they're advertising 15 episodes as a treat when every TV show used to give us 24 until the pandemic.
author: @samfisher814

title: The Pitt | Official Trailer | Max
body: as a RN for over 30+ years, I absolutely wish everyone would watch this program! Usually my husband (also a RN) laugh at shows like this cause they are so freakin annoyingly inaccurate but it is the most and i mean THE BEST ACCURATE MEDICAL SHOW I HAVE EVER SEEN. it’s shows the good bad and ugly! In the 2nd episode I cried where i havent done in so long! From losing my sister when she was brain dead to my friends child who died from fentanyl! I wouldn’t wish that on anyone personally It’s time for people to see exactly what happens when/how long you wait  The abuse of staff when working, the long waits.
author: @Chemochemo143

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Season 2 Premiere

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Season 2 Premiere
body: I saw Noah Wiley interviewed last night on Jimmy Kimmel. Not wearing the helmet was purposeful, especially regarding what the character is experiencing in his life.
author: @AnneSD

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Season 2 Premiere
body: I feel the show is hinting at Dr. Robby having Suicidal Ideation, he isn’t actively planning his death nor does he necessarily want to die, but he also isn’t against death and isn’t valuing his life. He’s putting himself in callous danger in little ways, like not wearing his helmet, because he’s not as opposed to dying as he should be. Really interesting thing for them to attempt tackling
author: @calicomarker

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Season 2 Premiere
body: @ 9:50 I like that he's not entertaining her nonsense, as soon as she brings up her new 'project' for the hospital. Like ma'am, you just got here, slow your damn roll. 😂

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Season 2 Premiere
body: Not in health care but I have seen it in many situations where a new manager starts making changes before understanding how the old system worked, and it's a disaster

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Season 2 Premiere
body: Fun fact, "gunner" is also used in law school as an annoying, know-it-all, overachieving student, who may or may not be competent, and who everyone wants to kick.
author: @JustaTyson

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Season 2 Premiere
body: They definitely gave the B12 as a placebo, not because she might have a hx of deficiency, she is clearly a frequent flyer as he knows her by name as soon as he sees her, and they know she won't calm down until they do something, so they give her a b12 shot....I think a lot of us have had to resort to placebos, I had a patient that wouldn't wake up, all tests and scans normal, she wouldn't even respond to pain, neurologist came in and said aloud "we are giving her a medication to help her wake up" and I pushed saline IV (that was the plan before going in the room) and she immediately woke up
author: @TotallyMarisa

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Season 2 Premiere

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Season 2 Premiere
body:
I knew two things when I saw the episode.

1) Mike would be calling out the lack of helmet.
2) Mike would be so focused on the lack of helmet he wouldn't remember Robby's likely mental state.
author: @brentage5000

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Season 2 Premiere
body: Clinical pharmacist here!! Love the discussion of unregulated supplements and the dangers they pose. Encourage patients to get USP Verified supplements! They are regulated by the US Pharmacopeia (USP) which makes sure that they are appropriately labeled and adhere to the same level of testing as FDA approved medications!
author: @lovestephee

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Season 2 Premiere
body: My husband had dementia and was repeatedly asking for his medicine.  The Geriatric Nurse Practioner told me to get some  white Tic-Tacs and put them in an empty prescription container and just give him one to quiet him down (for awhile). It worked.
author: @elainegoad9777

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Season 2 Premiere
body:
When my sister kept getting injured the hospital had to ask about possible abuse. Reality is she has an autoimmune condition that was repeatedly being missed, despite my mum asking several doctors to look into it for years 🤦‍♀️

Mum was really upset, understandably so but it makes sense that they have to ask.
author: @J_M1l35

title: Doctor Reacts To The Pitt Season 2 Premiere
body:
Not just season 2, but renewed for Season 3. And in multiple interviews Noah Wiley stated more than once that they want to keep this going.

I smell a renaissance in medical shows. I for one am furiously excited.
author: @0vrsa0

title: ONCE UPON A TIME IN HOLLYWOOD - Official Trailer (HD)
body: That last scene where Sharon and her friends welcome Rick into her home really made me smile and shed a tear because, unlike in the actual real life events, they live. Quentin really took the events of that night and threw them out the window, and in doing so, he made a pretty special film. Loved every minute of it. Especially the final act.

title: Honest Trailers - World War Z
body: I lost it at "He's just your average every day, super attractive, scarf wearing, Spanish speaking, airplane piloting, sharp shooting, skullbashing, armor crafting etc etc stay at home Dad" :p Honest Trailers For the win!
author: @EnterTheAether

title: Honest Trailers - World War Z
body:
"World War Z was one of the most anticipated book adaptations this summer. Sometimes things are better left on the page."

Well, then it's a good thing they left most of it there, huh?
author: @OrionoftheStar

title: Honest Trailers - World War Z
body:
To sum up:
Basically filmmakers found out that 'World War Z' was a popular book, so they cooked up a generic zombie film and called the movie World War Z to put asses in seats, because the movie has zero to do with the events in the book.  

It's the same thing they did with 'I Am Legend.'
author: @kryssullivan4455

title: Honest Trailers - World War Z
body: He's just your super attractive, scarf wearing, spanish speaking, airplane piloting, sharp shooting, skull bashing, armor crafting, arm chopping, prisioner interrogating, surgery doing, slow-mo vision having, antidote making, eagle-eyed... stay at home dad 😂😂😂😂
author: @eliel9521

title: Honest Trailers - World War Z
body: “So many productive problems people were genuinely surprised when it wasn’t a complete disaster.” God I love this YouTube page. 😂😂😂
author: @jonstolte854

title: Honest Trailers - World War Z
body: They forgot the blind japanese gardener, The great panic, patient zero,  the China bits, North Korea and the catacumbs zombies in the awesome things from the book
author: @TheJas20

title: Honest Trailers - World War Z
body: The book World War Z was an awesome collection of horrifying interviews from various survivors of the zombie war which, at the time of the interviews, began 20 years before. I can tell you that the book and the movie have absolutely nothing in common except the United Nations being involved and the thing with Israel.
author: @mannersmakythman

title: Honest Trailers - World War Z
body: I'm glad I haven't read the book. I actually really enjoyed watching this movie. Especially zombies, who can outrun a fcking car.
author: @johnniewalker8818

title: Honest Trailers - World War Z
body: "all those reshoots must have cost a fortune wonder how thy paid for that" "ohh that really makes me want a coke" subtle
author: @sheepyjello

title: Honest Trailers - World War Z
body: To this day one of the best books I've ever read. The detail, the accuracy, the plausible theories and trajectory of the events that take place. So many different perspectives, cultures, failures, triumphs and unique stories stemming from the same event.. It's like Pulp Fiction but with less feet and more zombies.
author: @ederanged7960

body: He did hit that pitbox like a pro, both times exactly where it needs to be, middle of the box.

title: Brad Pitt Drives An F1 Car For The First Time | Full Day With McLaren
body: Not only is Brad Pitt not a pro race car driver, he's also 61. I know we all think "Giant movie action star, he's done stuff like this before" but he really hasn't, and very few people on earth have ever experienced what it is like to  drive an F1 car. It's physically very demanding and an assault on the senses. Props to him for doing this. He may look 38,  but his body most definitely is not.
author: @Allagi22

title: Brad Pitt Drives An F1 Car For The First Time | Full Day With McLaren
body: I swear if this is just 20 minutes of silly talking and random camera shots instead of an onboard laps
author: @adamgames1975

title: Brad Pitt Drives An F1 Car For The First Time | Full Day With McLaren
body: I respect Brad for doing this and actually learning to drive the car its self and really going all in. that's amazing..

title: Brad Pitt Drives An F1 Car For The First Time | Full Day With McLaren

body: There are reasons why this man is such a legend, and he just showed it here. He's dedicated, working hard, and isn't afraid to dive into new things. And that's beautiful to see, at 60yo he went all in with the F1 experience where he could have just played it in the movie and call it a day.

title: USA🇺🇸- Carnegie Mellon University, The Most Beautiful Campus Tour l 4K Drone
body: For being in an urban environment, it really is a beautiful campus with a tremendous architectural history. The Richardsonian Romanesque style that defines almost every building on campus is a style almost unique to Pittsburgh, and a collection of multiple buildings all in this style is truly amazing.
author: @cxa340

title: USA🇺🇸- Carnegie Mellon University, The Most Beautiful Campus Tour l 4K Drone
body: My daughter recently accepted their invitation at Carnegie Mellon, to enter their Doctoral Program (PHD).  She was accepted at UC Davis (CA), Duke University (NC) Dartmouth (NH) Georgetown (Washington DC) .  We live in North Carolina  but she had no interest in Duke.  Carnegie Mellon is a gorgeous campus.. Born in Ohio it will be fun to to return to this area.
